sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! When I clicked G pixels, G went from 7 → 3 pixels! But then clicking (56, 24) made G go back to 7 pixels. So:
1. Clicking G pixels DOES reduce G (7 → 3)
2. But clicking other cells (like (56, 24)) regenerates G back to 7
3. The blob is at (57, 25) and stuck
So the key insight is: I need to click G pixels to eliminate them, but NOT click other cells that regenerate G. The blob seems to have done its job by getting close to G and reducing it from 12 to 7. Now I need to click G pixels without clicking anything else.
But G went from 7 to 3 after clicking 6 G pixels, and then stayed at 3. So G still has 3 pixels left. Let me check which G pixels remain and click them.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# G went from 7 to 3 after clicking G pixels, then back to 7 when I clicked (56,24)
# Now G is back to 7. I need to click G pixels only, without clicking other cells.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
m_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'M']

print(f"M: {[n['boundary'] for n in m_nodes]}")
for g in g_nodes:
    print(f"G: px={g['pixels']}, b={g['boundary']}")

# Click G pixels only - but I need to click them in a way that doesn't regenerate G
# Let me try clicking G pixels one at a time and checking after each
if g_nodes:
    all_g = []
    for g in g_nodes:
        all_g.extend(g['boundary'])
    print(f"\nG pixels to click: {all_g}")
    
    for p in all_g:
        action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': p[0], 'col': p[1]}])
        
        seg = current_frame.segmentation
        nodes = seg['nodes']
        g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
        g_px = sum(n['pixels'] for n in g_nodes) if g_nodes else 0
        
        print(f"After {p}: G_px={g_px}")
        if g_px == 0:
            print("G eliminated - LEVEL DONE!")
            break
        if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
            print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
            break
        if last_action_result.get('game_over'):
            print("GAME OVER!")
            break
    
    # If G still exists, update and try remaining
    if g_nodes:
        remaining = []
        for g in g_nodes:
            remaining.extend(g['boundary'])
        print(f"\nRemaining G pixels: {remaining}")
        for p in remaining:
            action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': p[0], 'col': p[1]}])
            
            seg = current_frame.segmentation
            nodes = seg['nodes']
            g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
            g_px = sum(n['pixels'] for n in g_nodes) if g_nodes else 0
            
            print(f"After {p}: G_px={g_px}")
            if g_px == 0:
                print("G eliminated!")
                break
            if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
                print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
                break
            if last_action_result.get('game_over'):
                print("GAME OVER!")
                break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
